description: Fix resource leaks (WebSocket connections, MCP sessions) and flaky timing patterns in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts that cause cascading test failures and CI instability.

userStories.title: Wrap WebSocket connections in try/finally blocks in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want leaked WebSocket connections to be cleaned up on test failure so that subsequent tests don't fail due to the extension's connection slot being occupied by a stale WebSocket.
acceptanceCriteria: fakeWs WebSocket at lines 300, 345, and 542 is closed in a finally block (or before assertions that could throw)
rawWs WebSocket at line 735 is closed before the assertion at line 776 or in a finally block
All WebSocket close calls are wrapped to handle already-closed sockets gracefully

userStories.title: Fix MCP session leak and replace fixed 2s sleep with polling in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want the timeoutClient to be cleaned up on failure and the tab-close-during-dispatch test to poll for request arrival instead of using a fixed sleep so that tests are reliable under load.
acceptanceCriteria: timeoutClient.close() is called in a finally block around lines 504-566 so the MCP session is always cleaned up

The 'tab closed mid-execution' test (line 670-678) polls testServer.invocations() to confirm the in-flight request has arrived at the test server before closing the tab, replacing the fixed 2-second setTimeout
The polling pattern matches the existing pattern at line 520-532 (waitFor with testServer.invocations())

userStories.title: Tighten validation error assertions in dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts
userStories.description: As a test author, I want validation error assertions to check for specific Zod error content so that regressions in input validation are not masked by coincidental substring matches.
acceptanceCriteria: The assertion at line 597 checks for a more specific error indicator than just 'invalid' (e.g., the specific field name or Zod error format)
The assertion at line 614 checks for more than just 'url' (e.g., checks for 'tabId' or the specific validation message)
Tests still pass against the current server implementation
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/dispatch-resilience.e2e.ts

Lines 589-615: The browser tool validation tests use loose assertions:
- Line 597: expect(result.content.toLowerCase()).toContain('invalid') -- could match any error containing 'invalid'
- Line 614: expect(result.content.toLowerCase()).toContain('url') -- could match any error mentioning URLs

First, run the tests to see what the actual error messages look like (call the tools with invalid input and inspect result.content). Then tighten the assertions to match the specific Zod validation error format. For example, check for the specific field name that failed validation, or the Zod error code.
